mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling housing starts signals a rotation from cyclical construction to manufacturing efficiency, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when INDPRO and HOUST move in opposite directions, or forward returns show no significant difference between high and low signal terciles.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 1.5% compresses equity valuations by raising the cost of capital, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real personal consumption expenditures drives corporate revenue growth, directly supporting positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference between rising and falling consumption periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 105, supply chain bottlenecks emerge, increasing input costs and compressing margins, which drives negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to differentiate between supply-driven and demand-driven production spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in the federal funds rate signal an overheating economy where wage pressures outpace monetary control, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when UNRATE and FEDFUNDS move in opposite directions, or forward returns show no significant difference between high and low signal terciles.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, rising housing starts combined with stable industrial production indicate robust consumer confidence and capacity utilization, driving positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative in > 50% of instances where both series rise, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.
